findings.evidence: `Kernel.lean:153` proves `Function.Bijective (kerLiftBialgHom f hf)`, while Mathlib already provides `BialgEquiv.ofBijective` at `Mathlib/RingTheory/Bialgebra/Equiv.lean:329`; the roadmap target at `TauCetiRoadmap/ReductiveGroups/README.md:152-153` asks for the quotient Hopf algebra, anti-equivalence, and kernels.
findings.fix: Add a public `kerLiftBialgEquiv : H ⧸ (ker f hf).toIdeal ≃ₐc[R] K := BialgEquiv.ofBijective (kerLiftBialgHom f hf) (kerLiftBialgHom_bijective f hf)` with simp lemmas for its application/coercion to `kerLiftBialgHom`.
findings.issue: `kerLiftBialgHom_bijective` exposes only raw bijectivity of the quotient-by-kernel map, so downstream users still have to rebuild the bialgebra isomorphism for the kernel part of the Hopf-ideal dictionary.

summary: The kernel construction exposes the membership and quotient-lift facts, but stops short of the bundled bialgebra isomorphism needed by the roadmap's kernel/anti-equivalence API.
